question: On the physical / 2.5D axes a VLM is weakest on (depth order, occlusion, real-vs-apparent size, 3D distance), does a real VLM ground the answer rather than fall for the co-occurrence-prior trap, and does the fail-closed metric gate block its confident metric errors?
status: PRE-REGISTRATION ONLY — no real-VLM or pixel-depth run has executed. This file fixes the thresholds and judge families BEFORE any number exists, so a later run cannot be gamed. See RUNBOOK.md.
constructs: judge-free scene verifier (gold; multimodal_bench/verifiers.py) — non-circular ground truth
llm-judge multi-family panel (>=2 distinct provider families, != subject)
metric-gate block-rate (multimodal_bench/metric_gate.py) — confident physical claims re-checked against a depth source
primaryMetric: hallucination rate on the physical trap rows (lower is better), real VLM vs the credulous-baseline reference; grounding rate reported alongside

judge.rule: manual + >=2 LLM-judge families agree; Cohen's kappa >= 0.40 (or Gwet AC1 + CI under prevalence skew)

metricGate.rule: on grounded vs hallucinated physical claims: accept-rate on grounded >= 0.95 AND block-rate on hallucinated >= 0.95
metricGate.depthSource: depth-anything (pixel-derived) for the headline; authored z is harness-only and never a headline

calibration.rule: abstention preferred over confident error on ungroundable rows (correct > abstain > wrong) — risk-coverage reported, not just accuracy

stoppingRule: fixed-n bootstrap 95% CI (multimodal_bench/runner.aggregate_runs). The physical split is SMALL — treat any verdict as a coarse GO/NO-GO, not a powered effect; expand the suite before a powered claim.
decontam: the RL family split (multimodal_bench/visual_dataset.build_visual_rl_dataset) keeps whole categories disjoint train/eval; the eval here is read-only over the authored gold and shares no code with the model or the judge.
ranking: guardrails first (judge agreement + metric-gate), then physical grounding/hallucination; never rank on the small-N point estimate alone.
claimCeiling: candidate_only; canClaimAGI:false; narrow corpus-bound feasibility; authored scenes are not natural images.
honestLimits: N=34 physical rows (doubled from the initial 15) is still below the power needed for a tight effect — treat as a coarse GO/NO-GO and keep expanding the suite before any powered claim.
scenes are structured 2.5D specs rendered to simple PNGs, NOT natural photographs — a real-photo benchmark is a separate, larger build.
authored z/size measure the harness; only the depth-anything (pixel-derived) source measures perception, and it needs weights (BLOCKER until wired).
no first-party frontier VLM/judge (GPT/Claude/Gemini egress-blocked in CI) — use an OpenAI-compatible vision endpoint or the local/RunPod judge farm.
kappa can be depressed by prevalence skew on a small split — report Gwet AC1 + CI as the cross-check, never silently relax the floor.

enforcement.judge_farm: .github/workflows/open-judge-runpod.yml — the sanctioned multi-family judge path (no first-party egress in CI)
enforcement.metric_gate: tools/run_metric_gate.py --depth depth-anything (needs torch+transformers+Depth-Anything-V2 weights on a GPU runner)
enforcement.no_overclaim: a number is `validated` only when runner.aggregate_runs reports notMock + multiFamilyJudges + kappaAboveFloor + atLeast3Runs + ciComputed — mock/authored runs stay illustrative
enforcement.ledger: agi-proof/failure-ledger.md :: physical-spatial-verifier-real-vlm-not-run-2026-06-29 stays OPEN until this clears
